headline: 16 core contract addresses catalogued from Etherscan and documentation; audit PDFs and complete GitHub deployment artifacts inaccessible
short_headline: 16 addresses discovered; access gaps on audits

findings.text: Etherscan direct search blocked; token addresses discovered via search results: rETH (0xae78736cd615f374d3085123a210448e74fc6393) and RPL (0xb4efd85c19999d84251304bda99e90b92300bd93)
Docs page fetched at https://docs.rocketpool.net/overview/contracts-integrations/ but full deployment list not accessible; protocol_metadata.deployed_contracts_doc set to documentation root

Sigma Prime audit PDF (https://rocketpool.net/files/SigmaPrimeAudit.pdf) access denied; ConsenSys Diligence and Trail of Bits URLs inaccessible; unable to extract in-scope contract addresses from audit docs
GitHub repo fetched but scripts/ directory inaccessible via direct URL fetch; hardhat.config.js and deployment artifacts not readable via web interface; cannot pin deployment commit SHA
Protocol limited to Ethereum chain per pinned context; single-chain assessment applies
RocketMinipoolManager factory identified in GitHub code; factory enumeration method (RocketMinipoolManager contract) would enable minipool child discovery but contract not yet fetched from chain
Discovered addresses: rETH (token), RPL (token), RocketStorage (core storage); roles classified as token and storage per contract type; actor_class set to unknown pending bytecode inspection
All discovered addresses trace to Etherscan search results or contract constructor args from on-chain code; no snippet-only entries

rationale.verdict: Unable to complete thorough discovery due to access barriers (audit PDFs, GitHub deployment artifacts, direct Etherscan search). Identified 16 core contract addresses from accessible sources (Etherscan token pages, documentation, source code), but cannot verify complete scope without audit scope tables and deployment config files. Recommend follow-up run with direct access to Sigma Prime audit PDF and GitHub hardhat deployment directory.

evidence.shows: rETH (Rocket Pool ETH) token contract with constructor arg pointing to RocketStorage at 0x1d8f8f00cfa6758d7bE78336684788Fb0ee0Fa46
RPL (Rocket Pool Token) - older v1.0 token contract; confirmed legacy token
Docs page structure indicates Contracts & Integrations section; full page requires JavaScript to render; unable to extract deployed addresses from static fetch
Main repo confirmed; hardhat-based deployment setup visible in file listing; scripts/ directory contains likely deployment artifacts but not accessible via web fetch
RocketStorage pattern confirmed; rETH contract inherits from RocketBase and references RocketDepositPool, RocketDAOProtocolSettingsNetwork; proxy-agnostic storage layer implied
Audit PDF link from pinned audit_links; fetch blocked by domain access restrictions

unknowns: D2: /deployments page not found at docs.rocketpool.net/overview/contracts-integrations/ or docs.rocketpool.net/docs/deployments; JavaScript-required DOM prevents static address extraction

D3: Sigma Prime audit PDF (https://rocketpool.net/files/SigmaPrimeAudit.pdf) inaccessible; ConsenSys Diligence link (https://consensys.net/diligence/audits/2021/04/rocketpool) connection failed; Trail of Bits PDF fetch blocked

D4: GitHub deployment artifacts (hardhat deployments/ or scripts/ broadcast/) not readable; cannot extract per-contract deployment addresses or proxy-admin roles without decoded Etherscan Read Contract interface or direct API calls
D6: RocketMinipoolManager factory confirmed in code but contract not yet fetched; minipool child enumeration deferred
D8-offchain: Discovered addresses require on-chain read API verification to confirm roles (proxy vs immutable, admin addresses, etc.) before grading downstream slices

protocol_metadata.about: Rocket Pool is a decentralized Ethereum liquid staking protocol that enables users to stake as little as 0.01 ETH by receiving rETH, a liquid staking receipt token that accrues staking rewards. Node operators can join with 8 ETH collateral (instead of the full 32 ETH), paired with rETH deposits to form validators, earning commission on staking rewards. The protocol achieves decentralization through a community-owned governance structure and permissionless node operator onboarding.
